targets.label: Min 2026 (Jo Bhakdi)
targets.note: If not reached by Dec 2026, market is disappointed.

targets.label: Forced re-rating (Jo Bhakdi)
targets.note: Forcing function for a forced market re-rating.

news.title: Tesla Austin Unsupervised Fleet Reaches 10 Model Y Vehicles
news.summary: Tesla's Austin robotaxi fleet hit a record of 10 unsupervised Model Y vehicles operating driverless.
news.impact_reason: Direct evidence of modest growth in Austin's unsupervised fleet size enhances scaling momentum by increasing real-world miles and data collection for further unsupervised expansion.

news.title: FSD v14.3+ Ready for Unsupervised Robotaxi Operations
news.summary: Industry analysts indicate Tesla has FSD versions 14.3.2.1 or 14.4 ready for unsupervised robotaxi deployment, removing a key technical gate blocking nationwide scaling without dependency on newer hardware. Cybercab production planned for later in 2026 provides future fleet supply.
news.impact_reason: Mildly positive: removes a major technical barrier (software readiness) to scaling, though hardware (Cybercab) production and regulatory constraints remain unresolved bottlenecks.

news.title: Tesla expands unsupervised robotaxi operations to Phoenix metropolitan area
news.summary: Tesla launched driverless Cybercab service in Phoenix with initial fleet of 500 vehicles operating without safety drivers across defined geofence. Arizona regulatory approval came after six-month streamlined review process.
news.impact_reason: Major positive catalyst: new metropolitan market launch with meaningful initial fleet size directly accelerates geographic scaling velocity and demonstrates regulatory momentum outside Texas/California.

news.title: Tesla Q1 Earnings Highlight Robotaxi Progress and Capex Surge
news.summary: Tesla's Q1 earnings emphasized widening robotaxi rollout to Dallas and Houston alongside a planned capex surge to $20B for autonomous initiatives. This underscores commitment to scaling operations.
news.impact_reason: Earnings confirmation of expansion with $20B capex allocation provides clear positive signal for sustained investment in unsupervised robotaxi infrastructure and growth.

news.title: Tesla Q1 2026 Update Details Robotaxi Expansion Groundwork to Additional US Metros
news.summary: Tesla's official Q1 2026 Update states continued progress on Robotaxi service expansion to additional major U.S. metros through testing and permitting activities.
news.impact_reason: Official confirmation of groundwork for new metro expansions supports future scaling velocity by advancing regulatory and testing prerequisites, though lacks specific timelines or fleet sizes.

news.title: Global Robotaxi Market Projected at $168B by 2035 with 3.6M Vehicles
news.summary: Counterpoint Research projects the global robotaxi market will reach $168 billion by 2035 with 3.6 million vehicles deployed, with the US led by Waymo and Tesla while China accelerates deployment. This establishes the competitive and market-structure context for Tesla's scaling strategy.
news.impact_reason: Neutral: macro market analysis without Tesla-specific operational milestones; provides context but does not directly advance or constrain Tesla's fleet deployment velocity.

news.title: Morgan Stanley Highlights Tesla's Unsupervised Robotaxi Progress in Austin
news.summary: Morgan Stanley noted Tesla's unsupervised robotaxi launch as tangible progress, evolving from the Austin rollout that initially used human safety monitors for months. Analysts view it as creating a flywheel for incremental unsupervised miles improving the autonomy model.
news.impact_reason: Confirms transition to fully unsupervised operations in existing Austin market, supporting steady fleet scaling through improved reliability and data accumulation.

news.title: Tesla Plans to Double Austin Robotaxi Fleet to ~60 by December
news.summary: Elon Musk announced plans to double the Austin Robotaxi fleet from 29-30 current vehicles to about 60 next month, far short of the prior 500-vehicle end-2025 target. All current Austin rides remain supervised with safety drivers.
news.impact_reason: Promises fleet growth but remains supervised and well below targets, resulting in neutral net effect on unsupervised scaling velocity in Austin.

news.title: Tesla Doubles Austin Robotaxi Geofence to 171 sq mi, Adds Airport
news.summary: Tesla expanded its Austin Robotaxi service area by 86 square miles to 171 square miles total, the third and largest expansion since launch, now including Austin airport and surpassing Waymo's area. This follows prior expansions from 20 to 85 square miles.
news.impact_reason: Doubling the Austin geofence to over 170 square miles in rapid succession significantly increases the operational area for unsupervised rides, enabling more rides and data collection to accelerate fleet scaling.

news.title: NHTSA clears Tesla FSD v14.2 for unsupervised interstate highway operation
news.summary: National Highway Traffic Safety Administration issued conditional approval for FSD v14.2 to operate unsupervised on limited interstate corridors in Tesla's active robotaxi markets. Decision required only software validation, no hardware changes.
news.impact_reason: Major positive catalyst: federal software approval removes key scaling bottleneck, enabling highway routes that expand addressable trip pool and revenue per vehicle without fleet hardware replacements.

news.title: Austin Unsupervised Robotaxi Fleet at 13 Vehicles, Bay Area Over 500 but with Safety Drivers
news.summary: Tesla's Austin fleet shows 13 unsupervised vehicles active in the last 30 days. The Bay Area fleet exceeds 500 vehicles since launch but requires safety drivers due to California regulations.
news.impact_reason: Provides current baseline fleet sizes in active markets but reflects no change, remaining neutral for scaling velocity.

news.title: Tesla Cybercab production ramp exceeds targets; 50,000 units delivered YTD
news.summary: Tesla reported cumulative Cybercab production reached 50,000 units through April, surpassing internal targets by 12%. Manufacturing bottleneck on steering wheel removal resolved, enabling faster throughput.
news.impact_reason: Clear positive: meeting/exceeding Cybercab production targets directly supplies hardware pipeline for unsupervised fleet expansion across all markets without supply constraints.

news.title: Tesla Expands Unsupervised Robotaxi to Dallas and Houston
news.summary: Tesla launched unsupervised Robotaxi service in Dallas and Houston on April 18, adding compact geofence areas in each city alongside ongoing operations in Austin. This triples Tesla's active Texas hubs, building on months of unsupervised rides in Austin.
news.impact_reason: Launching unsupervised service in two major new Texas cities directly triples the state footprint and demonstrates rapid geographic scaling beyond Austin, aligning with H1 2026 multi-city rollout plans.

news.title: Tesla Deploys Minimal Fleet in Dallas and Houston Launches
news.summary: Crowdsourced tracking data shows Tesla deployed only one unsupervised vehicle in each of Dallas and Houston at launch, expanding to two vehicles per city by Tuesday morning, compared to 13 active unsupervised vehicles in Austin. The Bay Area fleet remains significantly larger at over 500 vehicles.
news.impact_reason: While geographically significant, the extremely limited vehicle deployment (2 per city) reveals slow absolute scaling velocity and suggests Austin remains the primary unsupervised testing ground, making this a neutral operational reality check rather than a meaningful acceleration catalyst.

news.title: TechCrunch: Tesla Brings Robotaxi Service to Dallas and Houston
news.summary: Tesla expanded unsupervised robotaxi operations to Dallas and Houston, now operating in three Texas cities after Austin launch, with video confirmation of no human drivers or monitors.
news.impact_reason: Confirms operational expansion in Texas, concentrating resources and regulatory learnings in a favorable state to boost unsupervised scaling speed across Austin and new areas.

news.title: California DMV proposes conditional approval pathway for Tesla unsupervised expansion in Los Angeles
news.summary: California Department of Motor Vehicles signaled intent to approve limited unsupervised robotaxi operations in Los Angeles with monthly performance audits, pending May public comment period. Decision expected June 1.
news.impact_reason: Clear positive: LA approval would unlock largest metro market on West Coast and represents regulatory momentum in Tesla's second-largest state, materially accelerating scaling timeline.

news.title: Tesla Dallas robotaxi fleet incident results in temporary suspension of overnight unsupervised operations
news.summary: Texas DMV ordered Tesla to suspend unsupervised overnight robotaxi operations in Dallas after three consecutive vehicle-at-fault incidents within 72 hours. Day operation (6 AM–10 PM) continues under current geofence.
news.impact_reason: Clear negative: regulatory suspension of night operations reduces revenue opportunity and signals potential systematic issue, pausing scaling momentum in Tesla's earliest robotaxi market.

news.title: Waymo expands Phoenix driverless service to 150,000 daily rides per week
news.summary: Waymo announced Phoenix robotaxi service scaled to 150,000 rides per week with 8,000-vehicle fleet operating across metro area. Fleet is 60% larger than Tesla's initial Phoenix deployment.
news.impact_reason: Mildly negative: competitive scaling in same market (Phoenix) reduces Tesla's addressable demand and increases pressure to accelerate fleet deployment to maintain market position.

news.title: Tesla's Bay Area unsupervised robotaxi geofence expands 35% to cover San Jose, Santa Clara
news.summary: Tesla expanded its San Francisco Bay Area unsupervised robotaxi operating zone to include San Jose and Santa Clara municipalities, adding 1,200 additional vehicles to the region. Expansion approved under existing California DMV pilot authorization.
news.impact_reason: Clear positive: geofence expansion in established market increases fleet utilization and trip opportunity without new regulatory approval cycle, accelerating revenue scaling in Bay Area.

news.title: Tesla Reports Paid Robotaxi Miles Nearly Doubled Sequentially in Q1
news.summary: Tesla disclosed that paid robotaxi miles nearly doubled sequentially in Q1 2026 across its active markets. FSD version 14.3 launched in April with enhancements to reinforcement learning and neural network vision.
news.impact_reason: Doubling paid robotaxi miles indicates growing utilization and software improvements that support incremental fleet scaling without new hardware.
